notes: DOCUMENTED TARGET — not yet recordable (driver gap; see assessment.json). pi 0.70.0 rewinds IN PLACE: `/tree` opens an interactive tree-selector overlay; selecting an earlier entry calls session.navigateTree → SessionManager.branch(), which only moves the in-memory leaf pointer ('existing entries are not modified or deleted') and keeps the SAME append-only .jsonl session file under the SAME session_id. The next turn appends a child of the rewound entry to that same file. So the observable arc is: session born ready → turn 1 (ready→working→ready) → turn 2 (ready→working→ready) → user rewinds to the turn-1 checkpoint via /tree (NO observable transition — branch() writes nothing at selection time; the tailer cannot distinguish a rewind from forward progress) → turn 3 from the rewound point (ready→working→ready) on the SAME rollout session_id. The rewind itself is asserted ONLY as a negative invariant on the dwell window before turn 3 (no new session, no transcript_removed, no transcript_new). WHY same_session_as everywhere after t1_start: pi launches a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready that bridges until the UUID rollout transcript appears (same as the pi/multi-turn cell); the turn arcs must all pin to the rollout session, so t1_start anchors to recording start unpinned (first working ⇒ rollout) and every later phase pins via same_session_as. HOW runs differ: assistant wording, per-turn tokens, sub-second timing (model variance). Stable structurally: three ready→working→ready arcs on ONE append-only session_id, no new session ever created, a single process_exited, final state ready. RECORDABILITY: the /tree picker is keystroke-navigated (arrows + Enter) and drive-pi-interactive.sh has no `keys` step type, so this spec is the target a future driver extension must reproduce — not a currently-validated recording.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
First prompt → working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working)
First reply completes → back to ready; this is the checkpoint the user will rewind to
Second prompt → working again on the same rollout session
Second reply completes → back to ready

invariants: no transcript_removed for the rollout session
text: User opens /tree and selects the turn-1 checkpoint: rewind happens in place — session stays ready, no new transcript and no removal, no state change distinguishes the rewind
Third prompt, issued from the rewound point → working again on the SAME rollout session_id (in-place rewind, not a fork)
Post-rewind reply completes → back to ready; turn arc is clean from the rewound checkpoint
PID bound to the rollout session
REPL torn down once after the post-rewind turn → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
